name: Arc Poly
explanation: An American-style polyphonic synth voice: a bank of up to 8 independent voices, each with its own VCO (saw + PWM pulse), its own 4-pole resonant low-pass with a decay envelope, and its own amp envelope — so holding a chord sounds every note at its own pitch. A shared SAMPLE & HOLD source samples a new random level at the S&H Rate and wobbles every voice's cutoff by S&H Depth in lock-step, giving the classic stepped-random ARP burble. Pitch tracks each played note in Hz.
